• 🏗️ BMC processes 8,000+ complex building proposals annually covering over 40 lakh sq. m.

• 🧠 Platform uses 12 specialized AI agents for approval workflows, document review & submissions

• 🌐 Strengthens SoftTech's position in the AI-powered GovTech & urban infrastructure technology space

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major product innovation with a marquee government deployment, enhancing SoftTech's leadership in AI-driven digital governance solutions.)

HINDUSTAN ORGANICS ; HOCL EXTENDS TEMPORARY SHUTDOWN OF KOCHI PHENOL PLANT ⚠️🏭

• 🏭 Hindustan Organic Chemicals (HOCL) has extended the temporary shutdown of its Kochi Phenol Plant

• 📅 The plant was initially shut for 10 days from May 23, 2026

• 🔧 Shutdown extension is required to facilitate statutory testing of BPCL's LPG supply cross-country pipeline

• 🛠️ Company is also undertaking maintenance activities in certain equipment and pipelines during the shutdown period

• ⏳ Plant will remain shut until further intimation from the company

• 🧪 HOCL's Hydrogen Peroxide Plant continues to operate normally

🔴 Impact: Negative (Extended shutdown may affect phenol production volumes and revenue until operations resume, though continued operation of the Hydrogen Peroxide plant provides partial support.)
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
KRANTI INDUSTRIES FY26: REVENUE CROSSES ₹100 CR, DEFENCE ENTRY & EBITDA JUMPS 64% 🚀🛡️

• 💰 Consolidated Revenue crossed the ₹100 Cr milestone, rising 28% YoY to ₹100.45 Cr

• 🏭 Standalone Revenue grew 30% YoY to ₹93.88 Cr

• 📈 Standalone EBITDA surged 63.7% YoY to ₹12.44 Cr

• 🎯 EBITDA Margin expanded to 13.3% from 10.5% in FY25

• 💵 Standalone PAT turned positive at ₹2.60 Cr versus ₹0.75 Cr loss in FY25

• 🛡️ Entered the Defence Manufacturing sector with orders from Armoured Vehicles Nigam Limited (AVNL)

• 🏗️ Commissioned a new 35,000 sq. ft. Jaipur facility to enhance machining capacity

• 🚗 Revenue mix remains diversified with 64.7% from Automotive and 15.4% from Agriculture

• 🔄 Undertook strategic restructuring by selling 8 lakh Preciso Metall shares for ₹80 lakh

📢Back-to-Back Railway Wins: Magellanic Cloud Adds ₹7.64 Cr South Central Railway Contract
Magellanic Cloud Ltd (NSE: MCLOUD |  BSE: 538891)

* Magellanic Cloud's wholly owned subsidiary Provigil Surveillance Limited has secured a ₹7.64 Crore order from South Central Railway, Nanded Division.

* The contract is for the provision of IP-based Video Surveillance Systems across stabling lines, washing yards, and pit lines in the Nanded Division.

* Scope of work includes supply, installation, testing, commissioning, and maintenance of CCTV surveillance systems along with networking infrastructure.

* The project also includes a 5-year Comprehensive Annual Maintenance Contract (CAMC), providing a long-term maintenance component after project completion.

* The order is scheduled to be executed within 12 months.

* This contract adds to Magellanic Cloud's growing public sector e-surveillance order book, which exceeded ₹200 Crore during FY26.

* The project strengthens the company's presence in the railway surveillance and public infrastructure solutions segment.

* The order aligns with ongoing investments in railway modernization, digital infrastructure, and safety enhancement initiatives across India.

text:
Wockhardt Wins US FDA Approval for Superbug-Fighting Antibiotic Zaynich; Estimated $9 Billion Market Opportunity — India's CDSCO Also Clears Drug, Treatment Cost $10,000-15,000 in US, Significantly Lower in India

The Approval — What Was Achieved
Indian drug maker Wockhardt's breakthrough antibiotic Zaynich (cefepime and zidebactam) received novel drug approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA)

US regulator approved the drug for treatment of "complicated urinary tract infections, including pyelonephritis, caused by designated susceptible microorganisms"

Earlier, India's Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) had granted authorisation for import and marketing of Zaynich in India

Indian approval is for treatment of adult patients with complicated UTIs including pyelonephritis as well as cases with concurrent Gramnegative bacteremia

The Drug — What Makes Zaynich Special
Works on superbugs or bacterial infections that show resistance against existing antibiotic treatments

Being seen as a game-changing drug in treating drug-resistant gram-negative pathogens — pathogens that are difficult to kill

Market opportunity estimated at about $9 billion

Customer group same as for other Wockhardt antimicrobial drugs such as Emrok and Wck 6777 — used for treating drug-resistant bacterial infections in hospitals and intensive care units

The India Context — A Critical Need
India loses about one million people annually due to multi-drug resistance — four times the toll on account of Covid-19

Worldwide number is 5 million — twice as much as during the pandemic

CDSCO approval positions Zaynich as a potential solution to India's severe antimicrobial resistance crisis

Pricing — India vs US
Treatment cost in the US expected to be in the range of $10,000-15,000 per treatment of eight to ten days

Drug price in India would be significantly lower

Treatment cost for most new products launched in the US over the past 10 years has been in the $10,000-15,000 range per treatment — Wockhardt Chairperson Habil Khorakiwala

Core Theme 
Wockhardt's US FDA approval for Zaynich is a landmark moment for Indian pharmaceutical innovation — as the first novel antibiotic targeting drug-resistant superbugs to win FDA approval from an Indian company, it addresses a $9 billion global market opportunity while striking at the heart of one of India's most severe public health crises; with India losing a million lives annually to multi-drug resistance and CDSCO approval already secured, Zaynich's significantly lower India pricing could make this life-saving superbug fighter accessible at scale in the country that needs it most.
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

Cipla has tied up with MedTherapy ( Tata Sons-backed biotech firm) to brand and sell a new CAR-T therapy for blood cancer (like lymphoma) in India. 

This therapy is still waiting for approvals. MedTherapy says it can make the treatment in just 1 day (normally it takes weeks) and cut costs by more than 70%. It could reach the market around 2027.

India already has two approved home-made CAR-T therapies. This new one adds more competition, making treatment cheaper and faster for patients. 

CAR-T therapy is a one-time treatment where doctors take your own immune cells (T-cells from blood), change them in a lab to attack cancer, and put them back. It works very well for hard-to-treat blood cancers when other medicines fail. 

In the US it costs ₹3-5 crore per patient. Indian versions are already 10 times cheaper, this is Blessing 

Solid tumor plans - Most CAR-T works only on blood cancer. MedTherapy is also testing for brain, breast, lung cancers (harder area). Early hints look good.

Other countries still take weeks and charge millions. India versions are already ₹40-70 lakh. With this speed, India can export cheap CAR-T to poor countries and even compete with the US.

COAL GASIFICATION – HOW COAL BECOMES SYNGAS ⚙️

Step 1: Coal Preparation
- Raw coal is crushed and dried
- Coal is pulverized into fine particles
- Improves feedstock uniformity
- Enhances reactor efficiency

Step 2: Coal Slurry Formation
- Pulverized coal mixed with water
- Forms pumpable coal slurry
- Enables transportation through pipelines
- Suitable for high-pressure injection

Step 3: Oxygen Production
- Air Separation Unit (ASU) produces oxygen
- High-purity oxygen required
- Ensures controlled reaction environment
- Different from conventional coal combustion

Step 4: Gasification Process
- Coal slurry and oxygen enter gasifier
- High temperature and pressure maintained
- Coal undergoes partial oxidation
- Coal is converted into synthetic gas

Step 5: Syngas Generation
- Produces hydrogen (H₂)
- Produces carbon monoxide (CO)
- Produces methane (CH₄)
- Also contains CO₂ and other gases
- This mixture is called syngas

Step 6: Slag Removal
- Ash and impurities melt
- Converted into molten slag
- Removed from gasifier bottom
- Improves process efficiency

Step 7: Syngas Cleaning
- Raw syngas contains impurities
- Dust removed
- Sulphur compounds removed
- Carbon particles removed
- Gas is cooled and purified

Step 8: Downstream Products
- Ammonia
- Methanol
- Urea
- Synthetic Natural Gas (SNG)
- Various industrial chemicals

Simple Flow

Coal → Crushing & Drying → Slurry Preparation → Oxygen (ASU) → Gasifier → Raw Syngas → Gas Cleaning → Ammonia / Methanol / Urea / SNG

Key Insight
- Coal is not fully burned
- It is chemically converted into syngas
- Syngas becomes the building block for multiple chemical and fertilizer products
- This explains why coal gasification projects are highly capital-intensive and integrated industrial complexes

VIDYA WIRES – CTC BUSINESS UPDATE ⚡️

CTC Project Status
- CTC project currently under implementation
- No revenue contribution yet
- Commissioning targeted by Sep/Oct 2026
- Expected before Diwali
- Phase-1 capacity around 3,000 TPA

Industry Learning
- Entry into CTC manufacturing is gradual
- New entrants typically start with lower-capacity transformers
- Qualification process takes time
- Product approvals are critical before scaling

Progression Path
- Initial focus on lower voltage transformers
- Gradual movement towards higher voltage applications
- Eventually targeting 765KV transformers
- Long-term opportunity includes HVDC transformers

Key Takeaway
- CTC revenue contribution likely starts only after commissioning
- Capacity creation is the first step
- Customer approvals and qualification remain important
- Large 765KV and HVDC opportunities are longer-term milestones rather than immediate revenue drivers

India’s ethanol production capacity has nearly tripled, growing from 680 crore litres in 2018-19 to approximately 1,970 crore litres in 2025-26.

@BeatTheStreetnews

Under the current E20 blending mandate, the total fuel blending demand sits at only ~1,100 crore litres, plus an additional 300–350 crore litres for non-fuel (chemical and potable alcohol) applications.

This leaves a massive structural surplus. OMCs are currently absorbing only about 60% of the total ethanol capacity offered by distilleries, leaving overall industry utilization languishing between 65% and 75%.

States such as Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, and Karnataka account for the bulk of installed capacity, creating surpluses that range up to 277 crore litres in Maharashtra, while southern states such as Tamil Nadu face deficits of around 77 crore litres.

India’s fuel retail network of over 1.03 lakh outlets is predominantly configured for a single fuel grade, which limits the ability to dispense multiple ethanol blends. Storage capacity stands at approximately 77.8 crore litres, supported by around 313 centralised blending depots that handle over 1,000 crore litres of ethanol annually.

@beatthestreetnews 

Ethanol demand under the EBP programme to rise to approximately 1,200 crore litres by Ethanol Supply Year (ESY) 2026-27 and further to around 1,600 crore litres by ESY 2029-30, contingent on FFV penetration reaching around 5 per cent of new vehicle sales by FY28 and scaling up to approximately 20 per cent by FY30.

If FFV adoption is delayed, it was  estimated that demand would remain capped at around 1,200 crore litres in ESY 2026-27, rising modestly to approximately 1,500 crore litres by ESY 2029-30. Under such a scenario, ethanol demand would structurally undershoot current installed capacity by 20 to 35 per cent, keeping utilisation rates in the 65 to 75 per cent range for the foreseeable future. The report noted, however, that an upward revision of the blending limit to E25 could push utilisation above 80 per cent by FY28, advancing the absorption cycle well ahead of the base case.

Based on data from 24 rated entities with a combined capacity of around 350 crore litres and aggregate revenue of Rs 16,800 crore for FY25, Rating Agencies found that the majority of companies fall within the BBB rating category, with their credit profiles supported primarily by OMC offtake agreements and capacity utilisation levels. Entities rated A or higher are largely integrated sugar mills with forward integration into ethanol production.

In May 2026, the Bureau of Indian Standards formally notified fuel quality frameworks for E22, E25, E27., and E30 blends. This provides the chemical baseline to absorb the excess capacity.

The Structural Bottleneck: Even though the distilleries have the capacity to supply E25 or E30, India’s current auto fleet and fuel retail infrastructure are bottlenecks. Most older 2-wheelers and 4-wheelers on the road are validated only up to E20. Moving beyond E20 requires automakers to roll out Flex-Fuel Vehicles at a commercial scale, and fuel stations to upgrade their single-grade dispensing infrastructure to handle multi-blend fuel ecosystems.

Bank lending against gold is going up, and is 460,000+ crores! It's up 4x in 2 years now. NBFCs lend probably another 250,000 cr. India's monetizing their gold, slowly.

Physical jewellery demand is falling as prices rise, but financial gold demand (ETFs, digital gold, bars/coins) is surging. 

Indians still want gold exposure, they're just changing how they buy it.

If you look at both segments - gold/silver ETFs and gold-linked financial products - the brokers and AMCs sitting in the middle are the clear beneficiaries.

 Look at the YoY percentage growth. The volumes are there, the AUM growth is there. Nippon AMC is the foremost example of this playing out in real time.

NSE TRADING HOURS CHANGE – EFFECTIVE 3 AUGUST 2026 📊

Key Change
- F&O market closes at 3:40 PM
- Earlier closing time was 3:30 PM

Why The Change?
- New cash market closing auction introduced
- Auction session from 3:15–3:35 PM
- Helps determine official closing price
- Improves price discovery process

Benefits For Traders
- Extra 10 minutes for hedging
- Better position adjustments possible
- Reduced uncertainty at market close
- Better alignment with cash market closing

Other Changes
- Close price VWAP window: 3:10–3:40 PM
- Trade modification till 4:15 PM
- Pre-open timing unchanged
- Market opening timing unchanged

Market Impact
- More efficient closing price discovery
- Better cash and F&O market alignment
- Improved execution near market close

HPCL - Bullish Note

Morgan Stanley on HPCL – Tactical Buy; 50% upside

Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 610 

Believe the share price will rise in absolute terms over the next 30 days

Levered play on energy security and improving supply of crude oil

Net realization for fuel has seen its loss run rate reduce to nearly US$7-9/bbl outside of cooking gas

See potential reduction in crude premiums and improved EBIDTA quality as key triggers in coming weeks

@sTOCKSIP

Goldman Sachs on PhysicsWallah

Maintain Neutral

Target price raised to Rs 133 from Rs 130

Q4 revenue growth accelerated to 51% YoY, beating estimates

Elara Capital UPGRADES Sudarshan Chemical:

Upgrade to Buy from Accumulate
Target price raised to Rs 1,212 from Rs 1,047

Q4 recovery driven by acquired Heubach business.

Elara Capital UPGRADES Sudarshan Chemical:

Upgrade to Buy from Accumulate
Target price raised to Rs 1,212 from Rs 1,047

Q4 recovery driven by acquired Heubach business

BOFA ON INDIA CAPEX THEMES 🏗📈

• BofA says India's Shipbuilding sector is where EMS industry was around 7 years ago, indicating a potential long-term growth opportunity

• Brokerage views Shipbuilding as a new frontier of manufacturing-led growth

• Continues to like Data Centres, Shipbuilding & Transformer sectors

• However, believes transformer stocks now appear quite expensive after the sharp run-up

• Remains cautious on sectors heavily dependent on state government capex spending

Motilal Oswal on ONGC: 

Maintain Neutral Rating with target price of ₹265

Siemens In Focus – Negative – Two Downgrades

Kotak Securities on Siemens
Downgrade to Sell from Reduce; TP at Rs 2950

Not as much localized; getting hit, finding it difficult to alter
Results bring out the impact of limited localization, which is difficult to alter
Analyst call brings out aspect of timing issues in weakness in results
Guarded outlook on top of 8-10% ordering growth seen in recent past

Jefferies on Siemens
Downgrade to Hold from Buy; TP at Rs 4000

Subdued Margin Outlook
Lower FY26E-27 EPS by 23-34% to factor margin pressure
Believe there could be scope for downgrades if prices hikes are insufficient

SMR Jewels -  In Business of Designer Heritage Jewellery Business. 

Management Interview: https://youtu.be/thq6q1r7cCk?si=YuCRrS7_XedZf9ei

Analysis:
https://x.com/BeatTheStreet10/status/2059471570196975686?s=20

The company supplies heritage design jewellery to corporate jewellery players like HSJ, Rokde Jewellers, WHP, JOSCO Jewellers, Kalamandir Jewellers, Vaibhav Jewellers, and D. P. Abhushan Limited.

Raising Funds to repay debt, working capital, building Jewellery Studio (to be live in Approx 9 Months) to enhance design and customer experience.

From FY23 to 9MFY26
Financials
Revenue grew by ~ 5x 
EBITDA grew by ~ 13x 
PAT grew by ~ 20x
EBITDA margin expanded by almost ~ 3x to 8.65%
ROE at ~ 55%

Operational
No. of new designs/products launched grew by ~ 5x
Consistent increase in Average per customer order size
Trading revenue down from ~ 15% to ~ 1% to focus on owned manufacturing products

Going forward to focus on new designs, brand building, expanding B2B network, and operational efficiency

Valuations close to ~ 10.50 PE on 9MFY26 earnings.

Cantor on Adani Ports

Maintain Overweight; Hike TP to Rs 2178 from Rs 1719

Ambition 2031 Reinforces Integrated Transport Thesis
FY27 guidance is constructive yet intentionally conservative
Post-earnings message is that APSEZ remains a compounding infrastructure platform
Domestic ports remain the earnings anchor
International ports were a key upside driver
Logistics and marine continue to scale as strategic growth pillars
Ambition 2031 is the most important incremental disclosure
Capital allocation remains disciplined
Morgan Stanley on Ashok Leyland: Maintain Equal-weight; target price at ₹180
BofA initiates 'Buy' ratings on Lenskart with target price of Rs 575.

Jefferies downgraded Siemens India to Hold

Sees only 3% upside from current levels

**PINE LABS – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹185 from ₹260

**Q4 Performance**
- Core earnings missed estimates
- Revenue grew 17% YoY
- Contribution margin stood at 73%

**Profitability**
- Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 21%
- Lower operating expense growth supported margins
- One-time gains helped profit reach ₹585 Mn

**Cash Flow**
- Improved cash flow generation was a key positive

**Outlook**
- Management expects revenue growth to accelerate from Q1
- Growth expected to reach 20%+
- FY27 revenue growth guided at 21–23%
- Driven by higher POS deployment
- Supported by growth in issuing and affordability revenues

text:
**FINOLEX INDUSTRIES – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹220

**Valuation**
- Stock up 11% in the last 5 days
- Trades at 19x FY27E earnings
- Valuation in line with 10-year historical average

**Business Mix**
- Agri segment contributes 65% of pipes mix
- Viewed as a play on agriculture and rural demand
- Plumbing segment now contributes 37%
- Targeting ~50% plumbing mix over next 5 years

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- PAT exceeded Jefferies estimates
- Operating margin improved to 25%
- Compared with 14.6% in the year-ago period

**Volume Trends**
- Q4 volume remained flat YoY
- FY26 volume declined 4%
- Unseasonal rainfall impacted demand

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27–FY28 EPS estimates raised by 3–8%

**GROWW – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹225

**Management Meet Takeaways**
- Wealth management strategy
- Share gain opportunities in core broking
- MTF scale-up
- Customer behavior in consumer lending
- Regulatory developments

**Key Debate**
- Bulls appreciate execution capabilities
- Cross-sell opportunities remain attractive
- Bears question scalability of wealth management
- Regulatory risks remain a concern

**Valuation**
- Trading at PEG of 1.1x
- Risk-reward seen as favorable

Sar Televenture Limited (SME) 

Key Infrastructure Updates

Marks foray into Optical Fibre Business with India and UAE presence

1,800 4G/5G telecom towers completed

The company has completed the rollout of 1,800 telecom infrastructure towers for 4G/5G services.

85,000 additional home passes completed in H2FY26

BSNL is planning to set up around 112,000 towers for 4G and 5G services. 

SAR has signed Master Service Agreements with three major telcos, making its tower infrastructure ready for the tower-sharing model.

Since the towers are already built, sharing them with multiple telecom operators can help generate higher revenue without requiring significant additional capital expenditure.

Additional 60 4G/5G tower sites received from Vodafone

Results:-H2FY26 vs H2FY25

Revenue 280.35 Cr vs 207.21 Cr (+35.30% YoY)

EBITDA 47.34 Cr vs 32.34 Cr (+46.37% YoY)

EBITDA Margin 16.89% vs 15.61% YoY

PAT 36.22 Cr vs 28.52 Cr (+27.03% YoY)

**MPHASIS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**SBI CAPITAL MARKETS (SBC)**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,780
- Launched Tria platform to transition from services-led model to platform-plus-outcomes model
- Performance to be measured through Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR)
- Strong AI demand lifted TTM TCV above USD 2 Bn
- Supports high single- to double-digit revenue growth over FY26–FY28E
- Valuations remain reasonable
- Revenue growth trajectory has improved

**NOMURA**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,620
- Focusing on an “agency model” to compete in the AI era
- Large deal teams helping win more deals
- Delivery model being redesigned for AI-led execution at scale
- Maintaining disciplined capital allocation
- Continuing to invest for future growth
- Pivoting towards an outcome-based business model

**JPMORGAN**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,650

**Key Takeaways from Analyst Day**
- AI implementation in brownfield projects remains complex and requires deep enterprise context
- Launched AI platform Tria with subscription, implementation and managed services pricing
- Strategic engagement team helping expand small transformation projects into larger multi-year deals
- Following a balance-sheet intensive strategy of buying out contracts from tail vendors
- Transitioning from a services-led model to a platform-plus-outcome model from FY27
- Plans to report new metrics from FY28 including ARR, platform attach rate and net retention rate

**CITI**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,310
- Management introduced Mphasis Tria as the core strategy for accelerating enterprise agency
- Focused on helping clients convert AI capabilities into governed and economic outcomes
- Leveraging platform-led offerings to capture emerging enterprise AI demand
- Remains cautious on the IT services sector
- Continues to monitor AI's impact on long-term sector growth and valuations

**GMR AIRPORTS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**JEFFERIES**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹125
- Q4 EBITDA at ₹14.8 Bn vs estimate of ₹15.3 Bn
- EBITDA grew 47% YoY but declined 13% QoQ
- FY26 EBITDA rose 60% YoY to ₹60 Bn
- Growth achieved despite only 1% YoY passenger growth
- Q4 impacted by weaker international traffic
- Higher expenses at Hyderabad Airport weighed on profitability
- GAL platform EBITDA nearly doubled YoY in FY26
- Company reported full-year positive PAT after several years
- Net debt declined QoQ

**CITI**
- MarQFY26 core profit at ₹1.3 Bn
- Compared with loss of ₹2.5 Bn in MarQFY25
- Lower than ₹3.2 Bn reported in DecQFY26
- Core profit was 18% below consensus estimates
- FY26 earnings achieved 125% of Street FY26 estimates
- Reported NPAT included one-off gains
- Included ~₹1 Bn claim from Crete Airport
- Included ₹1.2 Bn reversal of Hyderabad tax liability
- MarQFY26 revenue was 1% below Street estimates
- MarQFY26 EBITDA was 9% below Street estimates

**Traffic & Non-Aero Revenue**
- Passenger traffic at key airports declined 1% QoQ
- Passenger traffic increased 1% YoY
- International traffic declined 2% YoY
- Domestic traffic increased 2% YoY
- Combined non-aero revenue grew 6% YoY
- Average passenger spending increased 5% YoY

**Airport-wise Performance**
- Delhi profit declined 47% QoQ to ₹1.2 Bn
- Hyderabad profit increased 108% QoQ to ₹1.8 Bn
- Goa losses narrowed 15% QoQ to ₹0.6 Bn

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt stood at ₹340 Bn in Mar-26
- Improved from ₹345 Bn in Dec-25

**SIEMENS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**HSBC**
- Hold rating maintained
- Target price: ₹3,540
- Order inflows remained strong
- Execution performance continued to be robust
- Higher material costs weighed on earnings in the March quarter
- Capex cycle remains steady so far
- Margin recovery may be more gradual than earlier expectations
- Current valuation adequately captures medium-term earnings growth and execution optimism

**NOMURA**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹3,700
- Order inflow grew 33% YoY in the March 2026 quarter
- Growth led by the Mobility (MO) segment
- Significant opportunities emerging from data center capex investments
- Commodity prices likely to pressure margins
- Rupee depreciation expected to create additional margin headwinds
- FY27F EBITDA margin estimated to be 73 bps lower than TTM levels
- Stock trading at 60x FY28F EPS

GMR Airports gains brokerage support after Q4FY26 results ✈️

• Jefferies: Buy | TP ₹125

• Citi: Neutral | TP ₹105

Jefferies highlighted 60% YoY EBITDA growth in FY26, full-year positive PAT after several years, and declining net debt.

Citi noted profit recovery and improving non-aero revenues, though Q4 revenue and EBITDA came in below Street estimates.

**ASHOK LEYLAND – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**HSBC**
- Hold rating maintained
- Target price: ₹180
- Management expects recovery from Q2FY27
- HSBC believes full recovery may take longer
- Most commodity cost impact likely to flow through in Q1FY27
- Near-term demand uncertainty remains a challenge

**CITI**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹206
- Q4 slightly above estimates
- Gross margin surprised positively
- Underlying demand remains resilient despite diesel price hikes
- Volumes could rebound in H2FY27 due to pent-up demand
- Export retail demand remains steady despite logistics disruptions
- Structural CV growth drivers remain intact
- Minor FY27/FY28 EBITDA estimate cuts due to commodity inflation and softer demand outlook

**CLSA**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹183
- Q4 EBITDA margin at 14.6%, in line with consensus
- Gross margin improved 82 bps QoQ
- Supported by January price hike and value re-engineering initiatives
- Raw material costs remain inflationary in Q1
- Company implemented ~1.5% price hikes to offset cost pressures
- Remains cautiously optimistic on M&HCV demand growth
- GST rate cuts and replacement demand expected to support growth

**JPMORGAN**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹175
- Q4 slightly missed JPM estimates
- EBITDA at ₹20.7 Bn (+15% YoY)
- Higher staff and other expenses impacted profitability
- Management did not provide explicit FY27 growth or margin guidance
- Demand drivers remain robust
- Fuel price hikes and disruptions may cause near-term demand dislocation
- Industry pricing discipline expected to continue
- Multiple price hikes likely across the sector to offset commodity inflation

text:
**ALKEM LABORATORIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹6,890

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue was in line with estimates
- EBITDA beat estimates by 11%
- Gross margin improved to 65.4%
- Gross margin expanded by 606 bps YoY

**Exceptional Items**
- Exceptional loss of ₹1.35 Bn reported
- ₹747 Mn impairment charge on real estate investments
- ₹603 Mn impact from changes in the Labor Code

**PAT Performance**
- PAT impacted by exceptional items
- Higher effective tax rate also affected profitability
- Exceptional charges are one-off in nature

**FY27 Outlook**
- FY27 guidance was in line with or ahead of current expectations

**Valuation**
- Trades at 23.6x FY27F adjusted EPS
- Trades at 19.7x FY28F adjusted EPS
- FY27F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹230.8
- FY28F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹276.2

**APAR INDUSTRIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹14,240

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Strong Q4FY26 performance
- EBITDA beat Nomura estimates by 3%
- EBITDA beat consensus estimates by 5%

**Conductor Business**
- Conductor EBITDA/MT grew 8% YoY in Q4FY26
- Demand remains structurally healthy

**Cable Business**
- Robust growth continues in cables
- DC cables offer premium pricing opportunities
- Strong demand outlook for DC cable segment

**Capex Plans**
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹15 Bn
- Investments aimed at supporting future growth

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27F EBITDA estimate raised by 7%
- FY28F EBITDA estimate raised by 9%
- EBITDA CAGR of 19% expected over FY26–FY29F

**Valuation**
- Trades at 37x FY27F EPS
- Trades at 29x FY28F EPS

**Investment View**
- Outlook remains on firm footing
- Strong demand across key business segments supports growth visibility

**VINATI ORGANICS – KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price: ₹1,200

**FY26 Performance**
- Revenue declined 1% in FY26
- Decline driven by lower revenues from IBB and ATBS

**Management Guidance**
- Management continues to guide for 15% volume growth in FY27

**Business Challenges**
- VOPL projects continue to struggle
- Market conditions remain a risk
- Competitive intensity continues to be a concern

**Earnings Outlook**
- Kotak reduced EPS estimates by a further 9–10%

**CUMMINS INDIA – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price: ₹6,000
- Stock considered fully priced in

**Growth Outlook**
- Demand drivers remain intact
- PAT CAGR of 18% expected over FY26–FY29F
- Domestic demand outlook remains constructive

**Q4 Performance**
- EBITDA beat estimates by 7%–9%
- Operating leverage offset gross margin contraction

**Margin Outlook**
- Near-term gross margin headwinds expected
- Commodity inflation may outpace pricing actions
- Margin pressure likely in the short term

**Valuation**
- Stock trades at 52x FY28F EPS
- Limited upside at current valuations

**ONGC – CLSA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- High Conviction Outperform
- Target price: ₹405

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- 4Q standalone PAT missed estimates by 22%
- Post-results analyst call revealed several one-off charges
- Adjusted core EBIT was in line with estimates
- Adjusted PBT was also in line with estimates

**Production Outlook**
- Management did not provide specific near-term guidance on production ramp-up
- Remains confident about new field development
- New fields expected to add more than 30% to gas production over the next two years
- Production additions expected to start from next quarter

**Mumbai High**
- Service contract with British Petroleum (BP) expected to provide support
- Likely to temporarily halt production decline at Mumbai High

**Valuation**
- Stock currently pricing in Brent crude at US$62/bbl
- CLSA sees over 44% upside using US$82/bbl Brent assumption for FY28
- Upside increases to 73% at current spot Brent of US$95/bbl

**BDL – GS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹1,260

**Execution Concerns**
- Execution remains a key concern
- Company missed expected execution for the seventh consecutive year

**Reasons for FY27 Miss**
- Domestic supply chain issues
- Unavailability of a component from an overseas supplier

**Revenue Performance**
- FY26 revenue declined 27% YoY
- Underperformed compared to other defence peers under coverage

**Inventory Build-Up**
- Inventory surged 75% YoY to ₹46.3 Bn
- Inventory exceeds 2x FY26 revenue
- Increase mainly due to finished goods awaiting delivery

**Order Book Strength**
- FY26 order book estimated at ₹257 Bn
- Implies >10x book-to-bill ratio
- Highest among companies under coverage

**Order Inflow Outlook**
- FY27 order inflow expected at ₹140 Bn
- Order pipeline remains strong

**Key Monitorable**
- Pick-up in execution will be the key factor to watch

**MCX – UBS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price raised to ₹3,600 from ₹3,200

**Earnings Outlook**
- Peak earnings momentum likely behind

**Volume Trends**
- Elevated volatility across key commodities in recent months
- Healthy Q1FY27-to-date volumes
- Volumes have normalized from Q4 levels

**Competition**
- Not worried about competition as of now

**Valuation**
- Stock trading at ~50x one-year forward P/E
- Around 10% above historical average valuation

**Investment View**
- Strong volume run-rate already priced in
- Limited upside seen from current levels
JPMorgan maintains 'Overweight' ratings on Mphasis with target price of Rs 2,650
Jefferies maintains 'Buy' ratings on GMR Airports with target price of Rs 125
Morgan Stanley maintains 'Overweight' ratings on Mphasis with target price of Rs 2,730.
Macquarie maintains 'Underperform' ratings on Alkem Labs with target price of Rs 4,900.
JPMorgan maintains 'Neutral' ratings on Ashok Leyland, hikes target price to Rs 175.
Citi  maintains 'Buy' ratings on Ashok Leyland with target price of Rs 205
Jefferies maintains 'Hold' ratings on Ashok Leyland, cuts target price to Rs 160.
Morgan Stanley maintains 'Equal-weight' ratings on Ashok Leyland with target price of Rs 180.
Macquarie maintains 'Neutral' ratings on Siemens with target price of Rs 3,510

**BROKERAGE HIGHLIGHTS: KEY INITIATIONS & TARGET CHANGES**

**High Upside Calls**
- Axis Securities sees 39% upside in Camlin Fine.
- Systematix sees 51% upside in Krsnaa Diagnostics.
- Systematix sees 50% upside in Orchid Pharma.
- Axis Capital sees 61% upside in Pine Labs.
- PL Capital sees 36% upside in IRCTC.

**Infrastructure & Industrial Plays**
- Motilal Oswal sees 26% upside in KEC.
- Motilal Oswal sees 23% upside in Syrma SGS.
- Motilal Oswal sees 13% upside in Astra Microwave.
- Motilal Oswal sees 31% upside in Zensar.

**Consumer & Retail**
- Anand Rathi initiated BUY on Cello World.
- Emkay initiated BUY on SJS Enterprises.
- Motilal positive on ABFRL growth momentum.
- Sharekhan downgraded ABFRL to Hold.

**Healthcare & Pharma**
- Axis positive on Jubilant Ingrevia CDMO growth.
- PL Capital cautious on Jubilant Ingrevia valuations.
- PL sees 18% upside in Narayana Hrudayalaya.
- JM sees limited upside in Artemis Medicare.

**Real Estate & Financials**
- PL sees 28% upside in Prestige Estates.
- Motilal positive on Kolte Patil revival.
- PL sees 23% upside in Kotak Mahindra Bank.

**Energy & Utilities**
- Geojit sees 24% upside in Mahanagar Gas.
- PL sees 8% upside in ONGC.
- Motilal cautious on Amara Raja margins.
- Motilal cautious on Hitachi Energy valuations.

**Technology & New Economy**
- Avendus highlighted India’s AI data centre boom.
- Yes Securities highlighted sector-specific opportunities.
- JM cautious on Aequs near-term performance.

**Final Takeaway**
- Brokerages remain selectively bullish on manufacturing, healthcare, infrastructure, electronics and energy transition themes.

**SATIN CREDITCARE NETWORK – STRONG RECOVERY & RETURN RATIO IMPROVEMENT**  

**Brokerage View**
- Mirae Asset Sharekhan maintained POSITIVE view
- Target price revised to ₹260
- Implies 16% upside from CMP ₹223
- Stock trades at attractive 0.6x FY28E book value

**AUM Growth Momentum**
- Consolidated AUM grew 18.7% YoY to ₹15,174 Cr
- Disbursements surged 42.8% YoY
- MSME business grew 59.4% YoY
- Housing finance business increased 38% YoY

**Profitability Recovery**
- Net profit jumped to ₹162 Cr from ₹22 Cr YoY
- PPOP surged 126.7% YoY to ₹290 Cr
- Strong growth supported by lower opex and better asset quality
- RoA improved sharply to 4.27%

**Asset Quality Improvement**
- GNPA declined 60 bps YoY to 3.10%
- NNPA reduced 140 bps YoY to 0.90%
- Credit cost improved to 2.06%
- Collection efficiencies improved across buckets

**Margin & Income Trends**
- NIM declined to 6.6% due to higher foreign borrowing costs
- Other income surged to ₹291 Cr
- Opex/AUM improved by 60 bps to 6.6%
- Stable NIM guidance of 12–13% going forward

**Subsidiary Growth**
- Satin Finserv AUM grew 92.5% YoY
- Satin Housing Finance reached ₹1,267 Cr AUM
- Housing finance business delivering strong traction
- Diversification beyond microfinance strengthening

**FY27 Guidance**
- Consolidated AUM growth guided at 25–30%
- Standalone AUM expected at ₹14,800–15,100 Cr
- Credit cost guided at 3–3.5%
- RoA expected to improve above 3%

**Long-Term Targets**
- Consolidated AUM target revised to ₹32,000 Cr by 2030
- Non-MFI business targeted at 30% of total AUM
- FY26–FY28 PAT CAGR estimated at 36%
- Average RoE expected around 16.7% by FY28

**Valuation Snapshot**
- FY28E PAT estimated at ₹614 Cr
- FY28E EPS estimated at ₹55.8
- FY28E RoA projected at 3.1%
- FY28E RoE projected at 16.7%

**Key Risks**
- Forex exposure from global borrowings
- Regulatory risks in microfinance segment
- Rupee depreciation impacting borrowing costs
- Sector-wide asset quality volatility

Source: Mirae Asset Sharekhan

**YES SECURITIES – US EARNINGS & MARKET UPDATE**  

**US Market Outlook**
- S&P 500 earnings momentum remains exceptionally resilient
- CY2026 EPS estimates upgraded from USD 296 to USD 339 over the past year
- CY2026 earnings revision trend at +10%, second-best upgrade cycle in 8 years
- Earnings fundamentals continue validating market rally beyond valuation expansion

**Mega-Cap Technology Trends**
- Magnificent 7 continue leading US earnings growth
- Aggregate revenue growth for mega caps at ~30% versus mid-single digits for broader market
- Nvidia remains standout performer with triple-digit revenue and EBIT growth
- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft continue posting superior profitability trends
- CY27 EPS growth for Magnificent 7 expected to moderate toward ~15%, converging with broader S&P 500 growth

**Sectoral Earnings Rotation**
- Industrials, Technology and Energy witnessing strongest earnings upgrades for CY2027
- Positive revisions seen in Dow, Intel, Seagate, Chevron and Devon Energy
- Signals improving cyclical recovery and capex-led expansion
- Energy and IT sectors show highest proportion of earnings upgrades

**Weakness & Downgrades**
- Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services witnessing maximum downgrade pressure
- Rate-sensitive and consumer-facing businesses remain under pressure
- Real estate-linked companies, cruise operators and media businesses seeing estimate cuts

**Market Breadth & Strategy View**
- Market leadership still concentrated in mega-cap technology
- Earnings breadth gradually improving beyond AI-led rally
- Selective cyclicals appear attractive due to improving estimate momentum
- Industrials, Energy and semiconductor-linked businesses remain preferred themes

**Key Data Points**
- S&P 500 CY27 earnings upgrades:
  - Magnificent 7: +4.4%
  - S&P 500 overall: +3.1%
  - S&P 500 excluding Mag 7: +1.4%
- S&P 500 Mar’26 earnings growth stood at 13.0% YoY
- EBIT growth for Mar’26 quarter at 12.6% YoY
- Revenue growth improved steadily to 7.5% YoY in Mar’26
